title: The quiet race to connect the last mile
dek: Rwandan engineers are testing community networks designed for the places conventional broadband misses.
author: Claude Uwimana
body: On a hillside outside Kigali, a solar-powered relay turns one connection into a network shared by homes, workshops and a nearby school.
Local operators handle maintenance, residents buy only the data they need and equipment is designed to survive unreliable power.
Reliable access changes what becomes possible—from price information for farmers to remote consultations at rural clinics.
